purpose: Evaluate whether DeepSpec/DSpark-style speculative decoding is promising for the Stage7 planner before training or integrating a draft model.
runtime_boundary: Agent_Planner only; this is an offline acceptance analysis and does not replace the current Stage7/Stage10 serving route.

source_project.repo_claim: Full-stack codebase for training and evaluating draft models for speculative decoding.
important_constraints: Default data preparation for Qwen/Qwen3-4B warns about roughly 38 TB target cache.
Default scripts assume a single node with 8 visible GPUs.
Domain-specific use should fine-tune the draft model rather than reusing released generic checkpoints.

draft_candidates.label: stage6_same_size_proxy
draft_candidates.decision: Rejected as a direct draft: too little agreement with Stage7 and same model size, so no practical speedup.

draft_candidates.label: stage9_same_size_near_draft_proxy
draft_candidates.decision: Promising as evidence that Stage7 outputs are predictable, but not usable as a speed draft because it is the same 3B-scale model.

draft_candidates.label: stage7_self_sanity
draft_candidates.decision: Sanity check passed; pairing and tokenizer comparison are valid.

decision: Do not run DeepSpec's default training pipeline in this workspace. If continuing Stage11, train or distill a small planner-specific draft against Stage7 outputs/caches under a reduced one-GPU cache budget, then run exact online speculative decoding on 128 smoke and 1k heldout.

promotion_gate.evidence_required: true online 1k heldout, not only offline acceptance proxy
